verses.text: 'I waited patiently for the LORD,' and He drew David up out of the horrible pit and miry clay, set his feet on a rock, and put 'a new song' in his mouth. Patient waiting was rewarded. God lifts the sunken soul and turns its groaning into praise.

'I waited patiently for the LORD,' and He drew David up out of the horrible pit and miry clay, set his feet on a rock, and put 'a new song' in his mouth. Patient waiting was rewarded. God lifts the sunken soul and turns its groaning into praise.

'I waited patiently for the LORD,' and He drew David up out of the horrible pit and miry clay, set his feet on a rock, and put 'a new song' in his mouth. Patient waiting was rewarded. God lifts the sunken soul and turns its groaning into praise.
'Blessed is that man that maketh the LORD his trust,' and God's wonderful works toward us are more than can be numbered. Trust brings blessing. The mercies of God are too many for any tongue to tell.
'Blessed is that man that maketh the LORD his trust,' and God's wonderful works toward us are more than can be numbered. Trust brings blessing. The mercies of God are too many for any tongue to tell.

God desires not mere sacrifice but an obedient heart: 'Lo, I come... I delight to do thy will.' These are the words of Christ, who came to do the Father's will. The obedience the law could not give, the Son supplied for us.

God desires not mere sacrifice but an obedient heart: 'Lo, I come... I delight to do thy will.' These are the words of Christ, who came to do the Father's will. The obedience the law could not give, the Son supplied for us.

God desires not mere sacrifice but an obedient heart: 'Lo, I come... I delight to do thy will.' These are the words of Christ, who came to do the Father's will. The obedience the law could not give, the Son supplied for us.

David has preached God's righteousness in the great congregation, not hiding His faithfulness and salvation. Mercy received is mercy proclaimed. Those who taste God's goodness should not keep silent about it.

David has preached God's righteousness in the great congregation, not hiding His faithfulness and salvation. Mercy received is mercy proclaimed. Those who taste God's goodness should not keep silent about it.
He pleads that God's tender mercies not be withheld, for innumerable evils and his own sins have overtaken him. He feels surrounded. Even the forgiven know fresh need of mercy day by day.
He pleads that God's tender mercies not be withheld, for innumerable evils and his own sins have overtaken him. He feels surrounded. Even the forgiven know fresh need of mercy day by day.
He asks God to make haste to deliver him and to confound those who seek his life. He commits his foes to God. The believer leaves both his rescue and his enemies in the Lord's hands.
He asks God to make haste to deliver him and to confound those who seek his life. He commits his foes to God. The believer leaves both his rescue and his enemies in the Lord's hands.
He asks God to make haste to deliver him and to confound those who seek his life. He commits his foes to God. The believer leaves both his rescue and his enemies in the Lord's hands.

Let all who seek God rejoice and say, 'The LORD be magnified'; though poor and needy, David is sure 'the Lord thinketh upon me.' His hope rests on God's mind toward him. However low we are, we are never out of God's thoughts.
